data.title: Easy guide to finding Mega Chimecho in Legends Z-A
data.description:
The Mega Dimension DLC for Pokemon Legends Z-A introduces an exciting new addition to the roster with Mega Chimecho, a Steel and Psychic-type Pokemon that’s quickly becoming a favorite among trainers. This powerful evolution brings enhanced stats and impressive battle capabilities, making it a worthy addition to any competitive team.

Finding Chingling in Hyperspace Wild Zones

Before trainers can obtain Mega Chimecho, they’ll need to start with its pre-evolution form. Chingling appears exclusively in the Mega Dimension DLC, which means purchasing the expansion is necessary to access this Pokemon. The tiny bell-shaped creature can only be found within Hyperspace Wild Zones, special dimensional rifts that appear randomly throughout the game world.


These rifts open for limited periods, creating a sense of urgency when they appear on the map. To access them, trainers must first complete the Hyperdimension Survey Missions, which serve as the gateway to this new content. Once unlocked, these zones provide opportunities to catch rare Pokemon that don’t appear anywhere else in the game.

Entering a Hyperspace Wild Zone requires special items called Donuts, which are prepared by Ansha and Hoopa. When a rift containing Chingling appears, trainers should act quickly to enter and attempt a catch before the window closes.


Evolving Chingling into Chimecho

  1. After successfully catching Chingling, trainers need to focus on building a strong bond with their new Pokemon. The evolution process requires high friendship levels, which can be accelerated by visiting cafes throughout Lumiose City. Taking Chingling to these establishments helps strengthen the relationship between trainer and Pokemon.
  2. Once the friendship level is sufficiently high, the final requirement is simple: level up Chingling during nighttime hours. The game’s day and night cycle plays a crucial role here, so trainers should plan accordingly. With these conditions met, Chingling will evolve into Chimecho, bringing trainers one step closer to the Mega Evolution form.

Locating the Chimechite Mega Stone

The Chimechite Mega Stone represents the key to unlocking Mega Chimecho’s full potential. Finding this rare item requires navigating a specific type of Hyperspace rift that’s more challenging than standard encounters.

Trainers should scan their maps for 2-Star Rifts marked with the designation Unidentified Mega Stone Detected. These special rifts indicate the presence of a Mega Stone guardian inside. Before entering, preparation is essential. Selecting a Donut with at least a +30 Hyperspace Level Bonus ensures adequate time to complete the challenge.

  1. Inside the rift, trainers face a Rogue Mega Chimecho in battle. This encounter differs from typical wild Pokemon battles, as the timer creates additional pressure. The calorie content of the chosen Donut directly affects how long the rift remains open, so higher-quality Donuts provide more breathing room during the encounter.
  2. Unlike other rift types that disappear after a failed attempt, Mega Evolution Rifts remain accessible. This forgiving mechanic allows trainers to retry as many times as needed without losing the opportunity permanently.
  3. Upon defeating the Rogue Mega Chimecho, it drops the Chimechite Mega Stone. Equipping this item to any Chimecho enables Mega Evolution during battles, activated by accumulating Mega Energy and pressing the right stick when ready.

Battle performance and optimal moveset

Mega Chimecho’s transformation brings substantial stat increases across multiple categories. Defense, Special Defense, Special Attack and Speed all receive considerable boosts, transforming this Pokemon into a formidable opponent. While it doesn’t quite reach the heights of powerhouses like Mega Metagross, Mega Chimecho holds its own in both player-versus-environment and player-versus-player battles, earning a Tier A classification in both formats.

The most effective moveset for Mega Chimecho combines Psyshock, Shadow Ball, Dazzling Gleam and Protect. This combination provides excellent type coverage while offering defensive options. Psyshock serves as the primary Psychic-type attack, while Shadow Ball handles Ghost and Psychic opponents. Dazzling Gleam covers Dark and Fighting types, and Protect offers crucial defensive utility.

Trainers seeking additional options can teach Mega Chimecho Steel Beam by locating the Secret TM vendor hidden within Lumiose City’s alleyways. This powerful Steel-type move adds another dimension to the Pokemon’s offensive capabilities, though it comes with recoil damage that requires careful management.

The moderate Speed stat means Protect can’t be spammed as frequently as with faster Pokemon, but the move still provides valuable protection during critical moments. Combined with Mega Chimecho’s improved defensive stats, this creates a Pokemon capable of handling diverse threats across various battle scenarios.

SOURCE: GAMERANT

data.title: AI tools automated my $120k job in 5 simple steps
data.description:
The realization hit during a particularly monotonous week. Eighty percent of work tasks followed predictable patterns that could theoretically be automated. The remaining twenty percent required actual human judgment and creativity. This distribution suggested an obvious solution: let artificial intelligence handle the repetitive majority while focusing human attention on the valuable minority.

The transition didn’t happen overnight. Building effective automation requires understanding which tools handle specific tasks best and how to connect them into seamless workflows. The initial setup demanded significant time investment, but the payoff became apparent within weeks. Tasks that previously consumed hours now complete in minutes, often with better consistency than manual execution.


The communication and data analysis handlers

Email and message management consumed roughly fifteen hours weekly before automation. An AI assistant now reads incoming communications, categorizes them by urgency and topic, drafts appropriate responses, and flags the few messages requiring personal attention. The system learns from corrections over time, improving its accuracy continuously. The key to effective email automation involves creating detailed response templates and decision trees.

Generating weekly reports previously required gathering data from multiple sources, performing calculations, creating visualizations, and writing summaries. Now automation pulls data automatically, runs predetermined analyses, generates charts, and produces written summaries in the company’s standard format. The entire process completes overnight, delivering finished reports by morning. The quality of automated reports often exceeds manually created ones because computers don’t make arithmetic errors or accidentally skip data points.


Schedule management and document creation

Calendar management alone used to consume several hours weekly. Coordinating meeting times across multiple participants, blocking focus time for deep work, and adjusting schedules when priorities shifted created constant administrative burden. Automation now handles this entirely, using AI to understand meeting priorities, participant availability, and project deadlines.

The project management automation tracks task dependencies, deadline approaching, and resource allocation. When projects fall behind schedule, the system automatically adjusts timelines and notifies relevant stakeholders.

Creating presentations, proposals, and documentation consumed significant time. An AI tool now generates first drafts based on brief prompts describing the desired content. While these drafts require human editing and refinement, starting from a complete draft rather than a blank page reduces creation time by approximately seventy percent. The automation excels at maintaining consistent formatting and incorporating company branding.

The integration platform that connects everything

None of these tools would work effectively in isolation. The fifth critical component is an integration platform that connects all the automation tools, allowing them to share data and trigger each other. When a new project gets created, the system automatically generates necessary documents, schedules kickoff meetings, sets up tracking systems, and notifies team members.

This integration creates workflows that rival or exceed human efficiency. The system never forgets steps, always follows procedures correctly, and operates twenty-four hours daily. Projects progress steadily even when humans are sleeping or focusing on other priorities.

Ethics and implementation strategy

Automating a job while collecting a full salary raises obvious questions. The justification lies in what gets done with the freed time. Rather than coasting, that time now goes toward strategic thinking, innovation, and projects that actually create value for the organization. The quality and quantity of meaningful contribution actually increased through automation.

Many companies are slowly realizing that they pay for results, not for hours of keyboard tapping. If automation delivers those results more efficiently, that’s ultimately beneficial for everyone. The alternative is watching competitors gain efficiency advantages while stubbornly insisting humans must do everything manually.

Start by documenting every task you perform for two weeks. Identify the most time-consuming, repetitive activities. Research which AI tools handle those specific tasks effectively. Begin with one small automation, refine it until it works reliably, then gradually expand. The biggest mistake people make is trying to automate everything simultaneously. This creates chaos when multiple systems malfunction at once.

This isn’t about replacing humans. It’s about elevating human contribution from routine execution to strategic thinking. Companies will increasingly value employees who effectively leverage automation to multiply their impact. Understanding how to build and manage these systems becomes more valuable than the ability to execute tasks manually.

data.title: GTA Online update brings mansions and mission creator
data.description:
Grand Theft Auto Online is getting its final major content update of 2025, giving players the chance to showcase their wealth through luxury real estate. Titled A Safehouse in the Hills, the new update lets players purchase sprawling mansions complete with premium amenities and customization options.

The content launched today at 10am GMT UK time, 5am EDT and 2am PDT for US players, and 11am CET for European audiences. The update arrives on PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X and S, Xbox One and PC simultaneously.


Players can purchase mansions through Prix Luxury Real Estate, featuring what Rockstar Games describes as sprawling outdoor spaces and meticulously designed interiors. The properties include a state of the art AI assistant to help with professional and personal tasks, positioning these homes as functional bases rather than purely cosmetic additions.

Mansion features and amenities

The new properties come loaded with features designed to appeal to veteran players looking to display their accomplishments. Trophy cabinets let owners showcase achievements while private salons provide entertainment spaces. Animal kennels, elegant garages with indoor car podiums, armories and vehicle workshops offer practical functionality.


Additional amenities include helipads for quick travel, cigar rooms, vaults for storing valuables, media rooms, arcade spaces and yoga areas. The variety aims to make each mansion feel like a complete lifestyle hub rather than just another property on the map.

Optional upgrades let players host their own parties and hire security teams, adding social and protective elements to ownership. These customization choices mean two players could own the same mansion layout but create completely different experiences through their selections.

Beyond real estate

The Safehouse in the Hills update extends beyond property purchases. Rockstar added new law enforcement vehicles and drift variants to the game’s expanding vehicle roster. Freemode events provide additional activities for players exploring the open world, while several experience upgrades improve quality of life across various game modes.

The most significant addition might be the new mission creator, described as an in depth and comprehensive toolkit. Players can design and publish custom missions using the system, creating objectives, placing actors and building scenarios limited mainly by imagination. This user generated content tool could extend the update’s longevity well beyond its initial launch as the community develops and shares creations.

Timing and context

Positioning this update as the final major content drop of 2025 signals Rockstar’s planning for the year ahead. The December timing lets players enjoy new content during the holiday season when gaming activity typically peaks. The wealth showcase theme fits naturally with year end celebration periods when people often indulge in luxury purchases.

The mansion system represents another layer in GTA Online’s evolving property empire. Players have accumulated apartments, businesses, bunkers, facilities, nightclubs, arcades and auto shops over the years. Adding luxury mansions creates a new aspirational tier for those who have completed other property investments and want something to work toward.

The AI assistant feature suggests Rockstar continues exploring ways to streamline player interactions with game systems. Rather than traveling to different locations for various services, the assistant concept centralizes access through mansion ownership. This quality of life improvement could influence how future content handles player convenience versus traditional mission structures.

Community creation tools

The mission creator tool positions GTA Online players as content developers themselves. Similar systems in other games have produced memorable community creations that sometimes rival or exceed official content. Giving players robust tools for building and sharing missions could generate thousands of new experiences without requiring constant developer intervention.

The toolkit’s comprehensive nature suggests Rockstar wants serious creators experimenting with possibilities rather than limiting options to basic scenarios. The more freedom players receive, the more diverse and creative the resulting missions should become. This could establish an ongoing content pipeline as talented community members develop followings for their creations.

The update’s combination of luxury showcasing, new vehicles, improved systems and creation tools aims to provide something for various player types. Whether someone wants to grind for a dream mansion, race new drift cars, host parties or build elaborate missions, the Safehouse in the Hills update offers multiple engagement paths.

data.title: Google, Apple may team up to ease Android-iPhone switching
data.description:
Switching between Android and iPhone has long been a frustrating process. Users often face missing photos, incomplete message histories, and apps that fail to transfer correctly. Now, signs suggest that Google and Apple may be working together to simplify this process, aiming for a smoother, more secure experience.

Early indications of this collaboration appeared in the latest Android Canary build, version 2512, which is available for Google Pixel devices. A new “Copy data” option has appeared under Settings → Profile → All services → Pair with iPhone or iPad. This expands on the existing eSIM-only transfer option in stable Android builds and points to a broader, more comprehensive solution.


A secure transfer workflow

Activating the feature initiates a wireless transfer process using a session ID and passcode. This acts as a secure handshake between the devices. The iPhone needs to run iOS 26, consistent with reports that Apple is preparing a compatible system for an upcoming developer beta.

The option also appears during the Android setup process if users select “Copy data from iPhone” and cannot scan a QR code. This suggests that the feature is designed to be part of the out-of-box experience, making it easier for new users to migrate without additional steps or apps.


While still in development, the feature indicates a shared protocol capable of supporting more types of data. Users may eventually be able to transfer photos, videos, messages, and other personal information without relying on third-party solutions.

Impact on device switching

If fully implemented, this collaboration could represent the largest improvement in cross-platform switching in years. Many users have hesitated to switch ecosystems due to concerns about lost data or complex processes. A polished and reliable solution may remove one of the biggest barriers to changing devices.

Google and Apple have confirmed that the initial rollout will be in Android beta versions before a wider release. While a full timeline has not been provided, the development signals a shift toward better interoperability and user-friendly device transitions.

Looking ahead

This joint effort may make device loyalty less about ecosystem constraints and more about user preference. By creating a more seamless experience, Google and Apple could redefine expectations for cross-platform migration, making it easier for users to switch phones when they want.

The move also reflects a growing focus on user experience in the competitive smartphone market. Users who previously avoided switching may find themselves more open to trying new devices without fear of losing important data.

The appearance of the new Copy data option in Android Canary builds offers an early glimpse of what could be a major improvement in device migration. While the feature is unfinished, it points to a future where moving between Android and iPhone is faster, more reliable, and more secure. As both companies continue development, users may soon find it easier to change phones without worrying about missing photos, messages, or other important content.

The collaboration between Google and Apple shows that even competing tech giants recognize the importance of user-friendly solutions, and it may finally make switching ecosystems a simple, stress-free process.

data.title: Fans crack the code behind Game Awards’ weird statue
data.description:
A mysterious demonic statue appearing in the Mojave Desert has dominated gaming conversations in the days leading up to The Game Awards on Dec. 11. Host Geoff Keighley fueled the intrigue through cryptic social media posts, leaving fans scrambling to decode what major announcement the monument might represent.

Early theories connected the statue to gaming giants like God of War, Diablo 4 or The Elder Scrolls. However, reliable industry insiders including Jason Schreier quickly debunked those speculations, sending the community back to square one. The answer may have finally emerged through an unexpected source: newly registered trademark filings in Europe.


Three fresh trademarks registered for the Divinity franchise have surfaced, featuring a logo and two icons that appear connected to promotional material. While the owner isn’t explicitly listed in the filings, the design unmistakably links back to the franchise created by Larian Studios. The modernized font suggests something new rather than a simple re-release of existing titles.

Icon matches statue’s glowing symbol

The connection between these trademarks and the desert statue becomes clear when examining one specific icon closely. The shape almost exactly matches the glowing symbol visible on the mysterious figure, creating a striking parallel that significantly strengthens the theory of a Divinity-related announcement.


An overlay comparison of the trademark icon on the statue image reveals remarkable similarity, moving the connection from speculation into strong evidence territory. Unless this represents an extraordinary coincidence, the statue appears definitively tied to the Divinity franchise.

The third trademark filing remains somewhat puzzling to observers. Initial thoughts suggested it might reference the Enwebbed or Spider Legs abilities from Divinity games, though trademarking such specific game mechanics would be unusual. Some fans speculate it could connect to the Black Ring faction from the series, specifically the God King character whose design potentially matches the statue’s lower half.

Industry insiders drop cryptic hints

Recent comments from gaming insiders add credibility to the Divinity theory. Shinobi602 remarked that while the reveal can’t please everyone, fans of the specific studio and genre will appreciate it. This description aligns perfectly with the complex RPG foundations that define the Divinity saga, suggesting an announcement tailored to a particular gaming audience.

Jason Schreier’s involvement in debunking alternative theories also carries weight. His track record for accurate industry information means his dismissal of God of War, Diablo 4 and Elder Scrolls connections should be taken seriously, clearing the path for less obvious possibilities like Divinity.

The Divinity IP appearing at The Game Awards wouldn’t be unprecedented. Sharp-eyed fans recently noticed a PS5 Pro Enhanced tag for Divinity: Original Sin II on the PlayStation Store. Age ratings for current-generation native versions surfaced as far back as last year, indicating work on franchise updates has been underway.

Divinity Original Sin 3 when??? I need it please…

— NeroJB (@JankoNero) December 9, 2025

What announcement makes sense

A simple current-generation update clearly wouldn’t justify the elaborate statue teaser and promotional buildup. The hype level points toward something more substantial, with Divinity: Original Sin 3 emerging as a leading candidate. Larian Studios has been working on multiple projects, including one codenamed Excalibur, suggesting they’re prepared for a grand reveal.

However, an important clarification emerged when the director of publishing at Larian Studios confirmed no plans exist for Divinity: Original Sin 3 specifically. This statement doesn’t eliminate a new Divinity game entirely, as Original Sin represents prequels to the main series rather than the franchise itself.

The Divinity universe encompasses five other games beyond the Original Sin titles, including Divine Divinity, Beyond Divinity, Divinity II: Ego Draconis and Divinity II: The Dragon Knight Saga. The lack of plans for Original Sin 3 leaves room for a new mainline Divinity entry or a different subseries altogether.

Franchise evolution suggests major move

The discovered logo features aesthetic elements from previous Divinity titles combined with refreshed, modernized typography. This design approach typically signals a new project rather than backward-looking remasters or ports. The visual evolution suggests Larian wants to honor the franchise’s history while pushing it forward.

A shadowdrop announcement of the Original Sin II current-generation upgrade would pair logically with revealing a full sequel. This strategy would give existing fans an enhanced way to revisit the series while simultaneously building excitement for what comes next. The trademark filings provide solid confirmation that a major Divinity comeback appears imminent.

Genre fans await specialized reveal

The specialized nature of the Divinity franchise explains why insiders suggest the announcement won’t please everyone. Complex, narrative-driven RPGs appeal to a devoted but specific audience segment. Unlike broad-appeal action franchises, Divinity games demand patience and strategic thinking from players.

For fans of Larian Studios and the genre, however, a new Divinity game represents exactly the type of announcement that generates genuine excitement. The studio’s reputation for deep storytelling, player choice and intricate combat systems has cultivated a passionate fanbase eager for the next chapter.

The timing aligns with Larian’s development cycle following the massive success of Baldur’s Gate 3. That game demonstrated the studio’s ability to deliver critically acclaimed RPG experiences that resonate with both hardcore fans and mainstream audiences. Returning to their original franchise with lessons learned from that triumph could produce something special.

Days until official confirmation

With The Game Awards scheduled for Dec. 11, fans won’t wait long for official confirmation. The event has become known for major game announcements, making it an ideal platform for revealing a significant franchise return. Whether the statue represents Divinity: Original Sin 3, a new mainline entry or something unexpected, the answer arrives soon.

The elaborate teaser campaign suggests Larian and The Game Awards organizers want maximum impact for this announcement. Building mystery through physical installations and cryptic messaging creates buzz that traditional marketing struggles to match. The strategy has certainly worked, dominating gaming discussions for days leading into the show.

Regardless of whether these trademark discoveries prove accurate, the speculation demonstrates the passionate engagement gaming communities bring to major industry events. The detective work involved in connecting trademarks to desert statues showcases how invested fans become in unraveling marketing mysteries and predicting what studios have in store.

Story credit: MP1st

data.title: Cellcom finally fixes service after frustrating outage
data.description:
Cellcom customers across eastern Wisconsin and northern Illinois woke up to communication problems early Tuesday morning after the carrier experienced a service outage that lasted several hours. The company announced it had fully resolved the issues by Tuesday afternoon, restoring voice, text and data services to affected subscribers.

According to an alert from Cellcom, a localized equipment issue caused intermittent service for some customers. The problem began around midnight Tuesday, with Down Detector showing numerous reports of voice service failures for the carrier throughout the early morning hours.


The outage prevented many subscribers from making or receiving phone calls, creating significant inconvenience for both personal and business communications. While some customers retained the ability to send text messages and access mobile internet, others found themselves completely cut off from all cellular services during the disruption.

Reports flood in from affected customers

Down Detector, a website that tracks service outages across various platforms, recorded a surge of complaints from Cellcom customers beginning shortly after midnight. The geographic concentration of reports painted a clear picture of the affected areas, with eastern Wisconsin bearing the brunt of the service problems.


Northern Illinois residents also reported connectivity issues, indicating the equipment problem affected infrastructure serving multiple regions rather than an isolated cell tower or local network element. The widespread nature of the outage suggested a significant technical failure rather than routine maintenance or minor glitches.

Customers took to social media and reporting platforms to express frustration over their inability to communicate, with many noting they had no warning about potential service interruptions. The overnight timing meant some people discovered the problem only when attempting to make calls in the morning.

Emergency services potentially at risk

The outage raised concerns about emergency communication capabilities, particularly given Cellcom’s previous service disruption in May that specifically affected emergency services. When cellular networks fail, the ability to reach 911 or receive emergency alerts becomes compromised, creating potential safety hazards.

While Cellcom’s latest announcement focused on resolving customer service issues, the company did not explicitly address whether emergency services experienced disruptions during this outage. The May incident highlighted how carrier problems can cascade beyond simple inconvenience into critical infrastructure failures.

Local emergency management officials and public safety agencies typically monitor cellular network status closely, especially after previous outages demonstrated vulnerability in communication systems. Redundant communication methods become essential when primary networks fail unexpectedly.

Pattern of problems emerges for carrier

This marks the second significant outage for Cellcom in approximately seven months, following the widespread May disruption that affected both emergency services and regular customers. The recurrence raises questions about the carrier’s infrastructure reliability and what steps have been taken to prevent similar failures.

Telecommunications carriers face constant pressure to maintain network uptime, with modern life increasingly dependent on uninterrupted mobile connectivity. Business operations, personal communications and emergency services all rely on stable cellular networks, making outages particularly disruptive.

The company’s description of a localized equipment issue suggests a hardware or software failure at a network facility serving the affected regions. Such problems can cascade through interconnected systems, causing broader disruptions than the initial failure point would suggest.

Technical challenges in modern networks

Modern cellular networks consist of complex, interdependent systems where failures in one component can affect service across wide areas. Equipment issues at central facilities or routing centers potentially impact thousands of subscribers simultaneously, even if the physical problem affects a relatively small piece of infrastructure.

Carriers typically build redundancy into their networks to prevent single points of failure from causing widespread outages. However, equipment malfunctions, software bugs or configuration errors can sometimes bypass these safeguards, resulting in service disruptions despite protective measures.

The intermittent nature of the service problems during this outage suggests the equipment issue created unstable conditions rather than complete network failure. Some customers experienced total service loss while others encountered sporadic connectivity, indicating the underlying problem affected different network functions inconsistently.

Customer impact and communication needs

For subscribers who rely exclusively on cellular service without landline alternatives, outages create significant communication barriers. Many households have abandoned traditional phone lines in favor of mobile-only service, making them entirely dependent on carrier network reliability.

Business customers face additional pressures during outages, as commercial operations often require constant connectivity for transactions, customer service and internal communications. Several hours of downtime can translate into lost revenue and customer dissatisfaction extending beyond the technical problem itself.

The varying experiences among customers during the outage created confusion about the scope and severity of the problem. While some retained partial functionality through text messaging or data services, others found themselves completely isolated from their mobile networks.

Resolution process and future prevention

Cellcom’s announcement that it had fully resolved the issues provides immediate relief to affected customers but leaves questions about long-term solutions. Carriers typically conduct post-incident reviews to identify root causes and implement preventive measures, though these internal processes rarely receive public disclosure.

The localized equipment issue description suggests a targeted fix rather than systemic changes across the network. However, the May outage combined with this recent disruption may prompt broader infrastructure reviews to identify common vulnerabilities requiring attention.

Customers affected by the outage will likely monitor service reliability closely in coming weeks, with heightened sensitivity to any connectivity problems. Repeated outages can erode customer confidence and potentially drive subscribers to consider alternative carriers if reliability concerns persist.

Regional carrier faces competitive pressure

As a regional carrier serving Wisconsin and surrounding areas, Cellcom competes against national providers with more extensive infrastructure and resources. Service reliability becomes a key differentiator in competitive markets, making outages particularly damaging to smaller carriers’ reputations.

The restoration of service allows normal operations to resume, but the incident adds to the carrier’s recent track record of disruptions. For a company serving communities across eastern Wisconsin and northern Illinois, maintaining customer trust requires not just fixing problems quickly but preventing their recurrence.

data.title: Another Cellcom outage leaves Wisconsin users in the dark
data.description:
Cellcom customers throughout Northeast Wisconsin found themselves unable to make calls or send text messages early Tuesday morning as another service disruption hit the regional wireless provider. The outage left numerous subscribers frustrated and disconnected, marking yet another episode of connectivity problems for the company.

Reports of service issues began flooding in shortly after midnight Tuesday, according to data from DownDetector.com, a website that tracks service interruptions across various platforms and providers. Multiple customers contacted the monitoring site to report their inability to access basic wireless functions, creating a pattern that suggested widespread problems rather than isolated incidents.


The wireless carrier has remained notably silent about the situation, offering no official acknowledgment of the problems through its website or social media channels. This lack of communication has left customers uncertain about the scope of the disruption, its underlying causes and when they might expect their service to return to normal functionality.

Understanding the technical impact

Affected customers have noticed their phones displaying SOS mode, a designation that indicates severely limited functionality. When a device enters this state, users lose access to their carrier’s network for standard operations including data transmission, text messaging and voice calls through the cellular network.


The SOS designation specifically means that while emergency calls to 911 remain possible, all other communication functions through the cellular network become unavailable. This creates significant inconvenience for customers who rely on their phones for work communications, family connections and daily coordination of activities and responsibilities.

Customers attempting basic troubleshooting measures have discovered that restarting their devices does nothing to resolve the connectivity problems. The persistence of issues despite this common fix suggests the problem originates from network infrastructure rather than individual device malfunctions, pointing to a systemic issue affecting Cellcom’s service delivery.

However, subscribers have found a workaround by connecting to WiFi networks, which enables access to internet-based communication services. Applications like iMessage, WhatsApp and FaceTime continue functioning normally when connected to wireless internet, providing some relief for customers needing to stay in touch during the outage period.

Questions without answers

Media outlets including FOX 11 have reached out to Cellcom seeking clarification about several critical aspects of the situation. Key questions include what technical issues or infrastructure problems triggered the service disruption, why only certain customers appear affected while others maintain normal service, and what timeline the company anticipates for complete restoration.

The selective nature of the outage presents a puzzling element to the situation. While some subscribers experience complete loss of cellular functionality, others in the same geographic region continue using their devices without any apparent problems. This inconsistent pattern makes it difficult for customers to understand whether the issue stems from tower problems, network routing complications or other technical factors.

The company’s silence stands in stark contrast to best practices in customer communication during service disruptions. Telecommunications providers typically acknowledge outages quickly through official channels, provide regular updates about restoration efforts and offer transparency about the causes once identified, all measures absent in Cellcom’s current response.

History of service problems

This latest disruption arrives just months after Cellcom experienced a far more serious and prolonged service interruption. In May, the Northeast Wisconsin provider suffered a widespread outage that lasted approximately two weeks, creating extensive hardship for customers who depend on reliable wireless connectivity.

That previous incident stemmed from what the company described as a cyber incident, suggesting malicious actors or security vulnerabilities compromised network operations. The extended duration of that outage raised questions about the company’s infrastructure resilience and its ability to respond effectively to serious disruptions.

The May cyber incident forced customers to seek alternative communication methods for an extended period, impacting both personal and professional activities. Businesses relying on Cellcom’s network faced particular challenges as they struggled to maintain contact with clients and coordinate operations without dependable wireless service.

The recurrence of outage problems, regardless of their causes, may concern customers about the reliability of Cellcom’s network infrastructure. While all wireless providers occasionally experience service disruptions, the frequency and duration of problems can influence customer satisfaction and retention rates.

Regional impact considerations

As a regional wireless provider serving Northeast Wisconsin, Cellcom plays an important role in connecting communities throughout the area. The company’s service area includes Green Bay and surrounding communities, where residents and businesses depend on consistent cellular connectivity for daily operations.

Regional carriers like Cellcom often differentiate themselves through local customer service and community connections, factors that can help offset the broader network coverage advantages held by national providers. However, reliability concerns can undermine these relationship-based competitive advantages if customers question whether their provider can deliver consistent service.

The timing of outages can significantly affect their impact on customers. An early morning disruption on a weekday potentially interferes with work communications, school coordination and other time-sensitive activities that require reliable phone service.

As this situation continues developing, affected customers await official communication from Cellcom explaining the nature of the problem and providing estimated restoration times. The company’s response to customer concerns and its transparency about the technical issues will likely influence how subscribers perceive this latest service disruption.

Until Cellcom addresses the situation publicly, customers must rely on WiFi-based workarounds to maintain communication capabilities while hoping for swift resolution of the cellular network problems affecting their service.

data.title: Instacart’s AI breakthrough: From chat to doorstep
data.description:
From recipe ideas to delivered groceries in one conversation—Instacart is revolutionizing the way people shop by bringing its entire platform directly into ChatGPT. The grocery delivery giant just became the first company to offer a fully integrated shopping experience with instant checkout inside the popular AI chatbot, meaning you can plan dinner and have ingredients on your doorstep without leaving the conversation.

The new feature works seamlessly with how people already use ChatGPT. Simply start your message with Instacart, can you help me shop for apple pie ingredients? and the app appears automatically. After signing in, ChatGPT helps identify what you need from nearby retailers, builds your cart with intelligent suggestions, and lets you pay and checkout without switching tabs or apps. Your order then gets shopped and delivered through Instacart’s network of shoppers.


Shopping just got smarter

This partnership between Instacart and OpenAI represents a significant shift in how consumers interact with both AI and grocery shopping. The integration draws on Instacart’s unmatched grocery expertise—the company partners with more than 1,800 retailers across nearly 100,000 stores, covering over 98 percent of households in North America. That means whether you’re searching for a specific brand of gluten-free pasta or looking for alternatives to your usual orange juice, the AI can actually find what’s in stock near you right now.

The technology behind this goes deeper than simple product listings. Instacart maintains a catalog of more than 2 billion product instances across its retail network, complete with real-time inventory and pricing data. This means ChatGPT can recommend items that are actually available on shelves today, not just products that theoretically exist. For a generation accustomed to AI hallucinations and inaccurate information, this real-world accuracy matters.


Three ways this changes everything

The Instant Checkout feature represents a major advancement in what’s possible within ChatGPT’s app ecosystem. When you’re ready to purchase, you can securely pay directly within the chat using your credit card through Stripe’s payment system. Digital wallets like Apple Pay and Google Pay are coming in the weeks ahead. The entire transaction—from browsing to payment—happens in one place, eliminating the friction that typically comes with moving between platforms.

Desktop and mobile web users can access the full experience immediately. iOS and Android users have the app available now, with Instant Checkout rolling out to those native apps soon. For iOS users, updating to the latest version of ChatGPT ensures access to all features as they become available.

Why this matters for your kitchen

The practical implications are substantial. Instacart shoppers, who earn by picking and delivering orders on flexible schedules, have access to hundreds of thousands of available workers. More than half the time, a shopper is already at or near your local store, meaning groceries can arrive in as little as 30 minutes. That speed combined with AI-powered personalization changes how people approach meal planning.

Instacart’s decade of shopping data powers the personalization engine. The platform understands your preferences—from dietary restrictions to brand loyalty—and applies that knowledge to every recommendation. If you’ve consistently bought organic produce or specific dietary items, ChatGPT learns that pattern and suggests accordingly. This transforms the shopping experience from a generic transaction into something tailored to your household.

What’s next for AI-powered grocery

This launch signals a broader transformation in how AI companies view grocery shopping. Instacart has already partnered with OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft to integrate grocery fulfillment into their platforms. As more consumers turn to AI for meal inspiration and everyday advice, retailers need reliable partners who can actually fulfill orders efficiently. Instacart’s real-time inventory understanding and massive fulfillment network make it the natural choice.

The company is also building AI deeper into its own platform through offerings called Smart Shop, which personalizes the shopping experience for individual users. Additionally, Instacart rolled out AI Solutions, an enterprise suite designed to help retailers leverage AI technology and innovation in an increasingly AI-driven marketplace.

Grocery shopping has long been one of those necessary tasks people want to complete as quickly as possible. By removing friction and integrating seamlessly into platforms where consumers already spend time, Instacart is making that task feel less like a chore and more like a natural part of your day. For anyone who’s ever thought about what’s for dinner while chatting with an AI assistant, this integration turns that moment into action—delivering the ingredients to your door before you’ve even finished planning the meal.

data.title: Is Google overtaking OpenAI? AI expert weighs in
data.description:
The competition in artificial intelligence is heating up, and Google is emerging as a strong challenger to OpenAI. Geoffrey Hinton, widely regarded as the godfather of AI, recently suggested that Google may now be surpassing OpenAI, a surprising turn after years of lagging behind in the AI race.

Speaking to industry observers, Hinton pointed to Google’s recent advances as evidence of its growing edge. The release of Gemini 3, an upgraded AI model, has been praised for excelling in reasoning and handling multiple types of input, a capability that many believe puts it ahead of OpenAI’s GPT-5 in several areas.


Gemini 3 and Nano Banana Pro boost momentum

Google has also introduced Nano Banana Pro, a lightweight image-generation model designed to run efficiently on devices without relying on massive cloud infrastructure. These two innovations demonstrate Google’s growing ability to combine high-level research with practical applications, increasing its influence in the AI sector.

This shift in momentum is striking compared with 2022, when reports suggested Google issued a “code red” in response to ChatGPT’s sudden popularity. Now, the industry appears to be watching OpenAI carefully as Google leverages its research and hardware advantages to make rapid progress.


Strength in research and infrastructure

Hinton highlighted Google’s end-to-end ecosystem, from proprietary AI chips to a global network of data centers, as a key factor in the company’s growing strength. Reports indicate a potential deal to supply these chips to Meta, which would reinforce Google’s position in AI hardware.

He also emphasized Google’s history of foundational AI research, including the development of the transformer architecture that underlies most modern large language models. Despite its long-standing expertise, Google initially approached public AI development cautiously. Hinton noted that caution stemmed from the 2016 “Tay” chatbot incident at Microsoft, which exposed reputational risks in releasing AI technologies too quickly.

Investing in future AI leaders

Hinton, who left Google in 2023 to speak more openly about AI risks, is continuing to influence the field through academia. Google recently contributed C$10 million to the University of Toronto to establish the Hinton Chair in Artificial Intelligence, a gift matched by the university. The initiative aims to support fundamental AI research, the kind that helped launch today’s surge in AI innovation.

As Google refines its AI models and strengthens its infrastructure, experts suggest the company could reshape the competitive landscape. Hinton’s comments underscore the importance of combining research, practical applications, and scalable hardware, a strategy that may give Google an enduring advantage over competitors.

The AI sector remains fast-moving and unpredictable, but the current trajectory suggests that Google is increasingly challenging OpenAI’s previously dominant position. With Gemini 3 and Nano Banana Pro capturing attention, Google appears to be translating its years of research and resources into tangible progress, marking a significant moment in the evolution of artificial intelligence.

data.title: How Xbox Game Pass members save $130 on VR headset
data.description:
Xbox Game Pass members received an unexpected treat this week when Microsoft unveiled an exclusive Meta Quest 3S bundle priced at just $249.99. The package represents a 34 percent discount from its usual $379.99 price tag, delivering $130 in savings to subscribers at every membership tier.

The announcement caught many by surprise as Microsoft continues rolling out holiday perks for its gaming subscription service. The deal bridges two major gaming ecosystems and offers an accessible entry point into virtual reality for Xbox’s massive subscriber base.


Complete VR starter package included

The bundle centers around the Meta Quest 3S, Meta’s entry-level mixed-reality headset that operates as a standalone device. The headset can also connect to a PC for more demanding VR experiences, giving users flexibility in how they engage with virtual reality content.

Included with the headset are Touch controllers that serve as the primary input method for VR games and experiences. The package also contains Batman: Arkham Shadow, a new story-driven game built specifically for VR that continues the acclaimed Arkham franchise. Beat Saber, the rhythm game phenomenon that remains one of VR’s biggest hits, rounds out the software offerings.


Additionally, buyers receive a three-month trial of Meta Horizon+, Meta’s subscription service that adds two curated games monthly alongside an expanded library of titles and member discounts. This trial gives new VR users immediate access to a rotating catalog of experiences beyond the two bundled games.

Quest 3S brings modern VR features at entry price

The Quest 3S offers color passthrough technology that enables room-aware augmented reality-style experiences. Users can see their physical surroundings through the headset while interacting with virtual elements, creating mixed-reality scenarios that blend digital content with the real world.

The headset delivers performance sufficient for current VR games while accessing Meta’s growing library spanning fitness, music, simulation, strategy and action genres. Beat Saber continues proving itself as a system seller, with independent estimates placing it among the top-performing VR titles to date. The VR Institute of Health and Exercise rates the game’s physical exertion roughly equivalent to tennis, appealing to players seeking active gaming experiences.

Early coverage of Batman: Arkham Shadow from gaming media emphasizes the free-flow combat system that defined previous Arkham games, along with stealth mechanics and gadget-based traversal familiar to franchise fans. Building a full Arkham experience for VR represents a significant exclusive for the Meta platform.

Simple redemption process for subscribers

Claiming the discounted bundle requires just a few steps for Xbox Game Pass members. Subscribers can navigate to the My Benefits section on their Xbox console, PC Xbox app or mobile Xbox app to find the Meta Quest 3S bundle offer.

After redeeming the offer, users receive a one-time-use link that applies the discount at checkout on Meta’s website. The promotion comes with limited supply, and each code links directly to the Game Pass account used to claim it, preventing transfers or resales.

Deal reflects growing ecosystem partnership

This bundle represents another step in the expanding relationship between Microsoft and Meta. Over the past year, the companies have increased their compatibility efforts, including allowing Quest headset users to play traditional games via the Xbox Cloud Gaming app on a virtual big screen.

Meta has simultaneously opened its platform to third-party developers through broader Horizon OS initiatives. This VR offer provides a low-friction entry point to mixed reality for Xbox users without requiring a gaming PC or console accessory purchases.

The context matters for both companies. Microsoft has disclosed tens of millions of Game Pass subscribers worldwide, making large-scale perks an important growth driver for the service. Meta consistently ranks as the leading VR headset shipper by volume according to IDC and other market trackers, driven by all-in-one devices with accessible price points.

Perfect timing for VR curious consumers

The bundle addresses multiple audiences simultaneously. First-time headset buyers get everything needed to start their VR journey without additional purchases. Families seeking approachable games and fitness-friendly options find value in the included software and trial subscription.

Batman fans specifically interested in experiencing an Arkham story built for VR have a compelling reason to make the jump. PC gamers with powerful rigs can take advantage of the Quest 3S’s ability to connect for tethered VR, accessing an even larger library of experiences beyond the standalone catalog.

The $249.99 price point delivers remarkable value when examined closely. Many VR headsets cost that amount or more without any included software. This package bundles the hardware with two premium titles that would cost around $60 combined if purchased separately, plus three months of Meta Horizon+ valued at approximately $24.

Rare convergence of hardware and content

Complete VR starter packages at this price point don’t appear frequently. The combination of capable hardware, flagship software titles and a discovery-focused subscription trial creates an unusually comprehensive introduction to virtual reality.

The $130 discount transforms what would be a significant investment into a more palatable expense for curious gamers who’ve been hesitant about VR adoption. Removing financial barriers helps expand the potential audience for both Xbox Game Pass and Meta’s VR ecosystem.

For existing Game Pass subscribers who view the membership primarily for its console and PC gaming benefits, this deal adds unexpected value to their subscription. The perk demonstrates how Microsoft continues finding ways to deliver benefits beyond its core gaming library.

Limited availability creates urgency

Microsoft’s note about limited supply suggests interested subscribers should act quickly. Holiday deals with restricted quantities often sell out faster than anticipated, particularly when they offer substantial savings on desirable hardware.

The timing positions the bundle as a potential holiday gift option for families and individuals. VR headsets have become increasingly popular presents as prices have dropped and content libraries have expanded, making this deal particularly well-timed for the shopping season.

This crossover promotion between Microsoft and Meta represents one of the most aggressive VR deals available this year, leveraging Game Pass’s massive subscriber base to drive Meta Quest adoption while adding tangible value to Xbox subscriptions.

Story credit: FINDARTICLE
